name: Castro Park
price: Free during scheduled open-play hours (minimum 16 hours/week guaranteed). Outside open-play hours, reservations are $8.25/hr for El Cerrito residents, $10.50/hr for non-residents, $21/hr for instructors (one-hour minimum; $1/hr fee for cancellations inside 48 hours), per the current elcerrito.gov Courts & Play Fields page. Several sources describing the July 2025 opening cite a flat introductory $8/$10 resident/non-resident rate, which appears to have been the rate at launch before a small increase to $8.25/$10.50.
hours: Free open play: Sat-Sun 9am-12pm, Mon-Tue 10am-12pm, Wed-Fri 4-6pm (summer) / 3-5pm (winter) -- reservations are not available during these blocks. Reservable 8am-sundown outside open-play blocks; not lit for night play. Park grounds open sunrise to 1 hour after sunset daily.
surface: Outdoor hard court -- 6 dedicated pickleball courts, converted from 2 former tennis courts in a 2025 renovation. Each court is individually fenced with an 8-foot perimeter buffer (a design response to the discovery that the original tennis courts were narrower than standard), with permanent lines.
skill: All levels welcome; each court is individually color-coded by skill level (green = beginner-friendly, blue = intermediate, red = advanced), per the local news account of the 2025 renovation.
weather: East Bay bayside flatland climate immediately north of Berkeley/Albany -- cool, breezy, and prone to summer marine-layer fog and onshore wind off San Francisco Bay, especially mornings and evenings. El Cerrito's hillside (eastern) neighborhoods sit above some of the fog that blankets the flats, but Castro Park itself is on the flatland side near the BART corridor, so it gets the fuller onshore-wind/overcast pattern; play typically warms and clears by midday even in summer.

name: Hall Memorial Park

weather: South Bay warmth near the salt flats — sunnier and drier than the Peninsula, hot summer afternoons, mild winters with little fog.

name: Peter Gill Memorial Park

weather: South Bay warmth near the salt flats — sunnier and drier than the Peninsula, hot summer afternoons, mild winters with little fog.

name: Milpitas Sports Center

hours: Offered as part of the Sports Center's multi-sport Drop-In Sports/Open Gym program, first-come first-served with no guaranteed spot (participants must be high-school age or older). Exact weekly pickleball schedule is reported inconsistently across sources and the city's own official page does not publish specific days/times: one aggregator says Tue 5–9pm & Fri 10am–1pm; another says Tue 5:30–8:45pm, Wed 8–11:30am, Fri 8–11:30am; an older (2023-era) source says Tue 5:30–9pm, Wed 8–11am, every-other-Fri 8–11am. Call 408-586-3225 or check the official page for the current schedule.
surface: Indoor wood court, permanent lines, portable nets

surface: PPA-quality court surfacing per an independent court-finder aggregator (CourtSource); the facility's own website separately lists 'Outdoor Surfacing' as an amenity bullet, which conflicts with that same page's own <title> tag ('Indoor Pickleball Courts in Milpitas, CA'), a contemporaneous Milpitas Beat news article ('premium indoor pickleball club'), and The Picklr's uniformly-indoor national brand — see notes, this is unresolved
weather: Indoor — no weather factor

name: Richmond Recreation Complex

weather: Central Richmond, a mile or two inland from the shoreline -- milder and less foggy than Point Richmond or the Marina Bay waterfront, though afternoon bay breeze and lingering morning marine fog still show up on many summer days.

name: Booker T. Anderson Jr. Park

weather: Inland Richmond near San Pablo Ave, far enough from the shoreline to mostly dodge the thickest bay fog -- expect more sun and less wind than the waterfront courts, though summer mornings can still start overcast before burning off.

name: Richmond Police Activities League

surface: Indoor gym floor (specific material not specified by sources checked)
